text: @pmarca A friend of mine showed me his OpenClaw setup. He runs open source models locally on his home computers for easier stuff, but spends ~$200/day on frontier models.
summary: @pmarca A friend of mine showed me his OpenClaw setup. He runs open source models locally on his home computers for easier stuff, but spends ~$200/day on frontier models.

parent.text: Magical OpenClaw experiences that use frontier models cost $300-1,000/day today, heading to $10,000/day and more. The future shape of the entire technology industry will be how to drive that to $20/month.
parent.name: Marc Andreessen 🇺🇸
parent.embed_html:
Magical OpenClaw experiences that use frontier models cost $300-1,000/day today, heading to $10,000/day and more. The future shape of the entire technology industry will be how to drive that to $20/month.

— Marc Andreessen 🇺🇸 (@pmarca) April 7, 2026

ai_summary: Marc Andreessen discusses the economics of frontier AI models, noting current costs of $300-1,000/day that may scale to $10,000/day, with the industry challenge being cost reduction to $20/month for widespread adoption. Elon adds practical context that power users combine open-source models run locally with selective spending on frontier models (~$200/day), illustrating the emerging hybrid infrastructure strategy for AI deployment. This thread directly addresses the critical business economics and technical architecture decisions shaping the AI industry's future viability and scale.
embed_html:
A friend of mine showed me his OpenClaw setup. He runs open source models locally on his home computers for easier stuff, but spends ~$200/day on frontier models.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 7, 2026
